solver: CPLEX (docplex; classical baseline), with scipy HiGHS cross-check

models.description: Classical (mixed-)integer linear program for rescheduling a light-rail / tramway line (Baltimore Light Rail) under initial train delays. Decision variables are integer arrival/departure times t_{s,j} of train j at station s and binary order variables y_{s,j,jp} resolving conflicts between same-direction trains. Minimizes total (weighted, normalized) secondary delay at objective stations subject to minimum headways, minimum passing/stay times and rolling-stock circulation. Built as an LP matrix in the LinearPrograming class and emitted either to CPLEX via docplex (make_ilp_docplex, used as the classical benchmark in classical_benchmark) or to scipy.optimize.linprog (HiGHS, integrality). This is the classical baseline against which the QUBO / quantum-annealing and QAOA formulations (in QTrains/make_qubo.py) are compared; only the linear model is transcribed here.

sets_indices.name: S
sets_indices.meaning: set of stations (e.g. PS, MR, CS); keys of the timetable dict

sets_indices.name: J
sets_indices.meaning: set of trains; odd-numbered trains run one direction, even-numbered the other

sets_indices.name: path_j
sets_indices.meaning: ordered sequence of stations visited by train j

sets_indices.name: P_same
sets_indices.meaning: triples (j, jp, s) of two trains travelling the same direction sharing station s (jp>j, common consecutive-station match), used for headway/order constraints

sets_indices.name: SP
sets_indices.meaning: triples (j, s, sp) of a train and two consecutive stations on its path, used for passing-time constraints

sets_indices.name: Circ
sets_indices.meaning: rolling-stock circulation pairs: dict {(j,jp): s} meaning train j terminates at s and departs as train jp (turnaround)

sets_indices.name: S_obj
sets_indices.meaning: subset of stations at which delay penalty is accumulated in the objective

parameters.name: a_{s,j}
parameters.meaning: nominal (timetabled) arrival time of train j at station s

parameters.name: p
parameters.meaning: minimum headway between two trains in the same direction at a station

parameters.name: stay
parameters.meaning: minimum dwell (stay) time at a station

parameters.name: tau_{s,sp}
parameters.meaning: minimum running/passing time between consecutive stations s and sp

parameters.name: prep
parameters.meaning: minimum preparation (turnaround) time before a circulating train departs in the opposite direction

parameters.name: dmax
parameters.meaning: maximum allowed secondary delay; also the normalization factor of the objective and the width of each t-variable feasible range

parameters.name: d_j
parameters.meaning: initial (primary) delay added to the lower bound of train j's variables; defines the rescheduling scenario

parameters.name: delta
parameters.meaning: extra passing-time increment added to running-time constraints for the stochastic / robust variant (0 in the deterministic baseline)

parameters.name: M
parameters.meaning: big-M used to linearize the disjunctive headway/ordering constraints via the binary y

decision_variables.name: t_{s,j}
decision_variables.meaning: (re)scheduled arrival/event time of train j at station s; bounded by its nominal time plus initial delay and dmax

decision_variables.name: y_{s,j,jp}
decision_variables.meaning: order/precedence variable for same-direction trains j,jp at station s; selects which of the two big-M headway disjuncts is active (created only when the two trains' time windows can conflict)

objective.expression_plain: minimize total secondary delay summed over objective stations and trains, normalized by dmax. Implemented as a linear objective with coefficient 1/dmax on each t-variable whose station is an objective station, plus a constant offset (-sum a_{s,j}/dmax) handled separately as obj_ofset.

constraints.name: headway_order_disjunct_1
constraints.indexed_over: (j, jp, s) in P_same with overlapping time windows
constraints.expression_latex: t_{s,j} - t_{s,jp} + M\, y_{s,j,jp} \leq M - p
constraints.expression_plain: if y=0, train j must arrive at least p (headway) before train jp at station s; if y=1 the constraint is relaxed by big-M (eq. paired with disjunct 2)

constraints.name: headway_order_disjunct_2
constraints.indexed_over: (j, jp, s) in P_same with overlapping time windows
constraints.expression_plain: complementary disjunct: if y=1, train jp must arrive at least p before train j; together with disjunct 1 the binary y selects the train order while enforcing the headway

constraints.name: minimum_passing_time
constraints.expression_plain: consecutive event times of a train must respect the minimum dwell (stay) plus running time tau between the two stations (direction-dependent: pass_time key is s_sp for odd trains, sp_s for even); delta adds slack for the stochastic variant

constraints.name: rolling_stock_circulation
constraints.expression_plain: when train j terminates at station s and departs as train jp, the departure (jp) must be at least stay+preparation_t after the arrival (j) — turnaround constraint

constraints.name: variable_bounds_and_integrality
constraints.expression_plain: box bounds on each variable (t in its nominal-plus-delay to nominal-plus-dmax window; y in [0,1]) and integrality; passed to docplex as integer_var_dict and to linprog via bounds/integrality. The lower bound shift by the initial delay d_j is what encodes the rescheduling scenario.

instances.name: Input_timetable.qubo_real_{1,2,4,6,8,10,11,12}t
instances.size: 8 hard-coded Baltimore Light Rail timetable instances of 1, 2, 4, 6, 8, 10, 11 and 12 trains over stations PS/MR/CS (and intermediate); each solved across delay scenarios delays in [{}, {1:5,2:2,4:5}]

instances.name: test timetables
instances.size: small worked examples (1-5 trains) with expected optimal arrival times and objective values

models.notes: Classical baseline only. The same line program is built once (LinearPrograming) and solved two ways: make_ilp_docplex -> CPLEX (classical_benchmark, solve_sched_problems.py:96-132, reports CPLEX engine name/version and wall time) and scipy.optimize.linprog(method='highs', integrality=...) (solve_on_LP, :65-93). The two are cross-checked for equal objective (:126,:148). Variables are declared as docplex integer_var_dict, so the CPLEX model is an ILP/MILP (integer t, binary y). The objective is split into a linear part (coeffs 1/dmax) plus a constant obj_ofset added back in compute_objective. Headway constraints use a classic two-disjunct big-M linearization with binary y; redundant y are skipped when train windows cannot overlap (LP_problem.py:60-64,199). Stochastic structure is represented by (a) the per-train initial-delay scenario d_j that shifts variable lower bounds, and (b) the delta passing-time increment for a robust/stochastic passing-time variant (delta=0 in the deterministic baseline). No explicit multi-scenario / two-stage recourse linear program is present in the classical code; scenarios are enumerated externally (delays_list in process_q_annealing.py). The QUBO/quadratic formulation (QTrains/make_qubo.py) is a separate quadratic encoding for quantum annealing/QAOA and is NOT transcribed here; its quadratic penalty terms are not linear and are intentionally excluded.

extraction_notes: Model taken directly from gurobipy-free docplex/scipy code in QTrains/LP_problem.py and parameters.py; constraint semantics confirmed against the assertions in tests/test_LP_encoding.py (e.g. headway rows [..,1,..,-1,..,M..] with rhs M-p and -p at :73; passing-time rows at :81-82; circulation row at :99-100). Math symbols (t, y, headway p, big-M) are inferred from code identifiers and docstrings; the repo README does not state a formal mathematical formulation and no equation numbers exist, so symbol names are reconstructed (recorded in source_symbol) rather than quoted. paper_reference is null: no DOI/citation for the formulation is given in README or code (README only cites a funding grant NdS-II/SP/0336/2024/01 and an MTA Maryland schedule URL, neither a paper). The objective offset (obj_ofset) is a documented constant term, not a separate constraint. Big-M default differs by call site (LinearPrograming default M=10; production q_pars.M=50). License file is named 'LICENCE' (British spelling) and is CC0-1.0.
